title: pelargonium × magnistipulatum
intro: Pelargonium × magnistipulatum, a species of flower known as the 'lemon scented pelargonium', is a hybrid plant within the family Geraniaceae, resulting from the cross between the P. tomentosum and P. citronellum species. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pelargonium × magnistipulatum include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
